soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7510 — fix: avoid stack overflow on deep logical filters
Xuanwo (core) · M (+107/-27, 2 files) · no forced escalation

ONE-LINER
  Deeply chained same-operator AND/OR SQL filters overflowed the Rust stack
  during Lance's SQL planning (recursive AST clone + recursive AST→DataFusion
  conversion). This PR flattens a same-operator logical chain iteratively, parses
  each leaf, and rebuilds a balanced (log-depth) DataFusion expr tree; and in
  sql.rs moves the parsed expr out of the AST instead of deep-cloning it.

SUBJECTIVE (author's framing)
  Explicitly a Lance-side workaround for OSS-1232. Body acknowledges the fix is
  scoped to the "known vulnerable path" and that a systematic follow-up should
  add shared expression depth/complexity limits across SQL / Substrait /
  DF-expr / merge-insert predicates. So narrow scope is intentional, not an
  oversight.

OBJECTIVE (what I checked, tied to ground truth)
  • planner.rs:343-366 flatten_logical_binary_exprs — iterative, explicit
    `stack`, no self-recursion. Same-op children are expanded in place; other
    nodes become leaves. Confirmed: for an N-deep same-operator chain this is
    O(1) recursion depth. This is the mechanism that fixes OSS-1232.
  • planner.rs:368-392 balanced_binary_expr — iterative pairwise merge over a
    VecDeque (`while exprs.len() > 1`), no recursion. Builds a log-depth tree.
    Empty-input guard returns invalid_input rather than panicking.
  • Leaf ordering is preserved: stack seeded `[right, left]`, pops left first →
    left-to-right pre-order; balanced merge pairs front-to-back. AND/OR are
    associative + commutative, so the rebalanced tree is semantically equivalent
    to the original left-deep tree. No operand-order hazard.
  • planner.rs:394-410 binary_expr — only the logical (AND/OR) arm is rerouted;
    every non-logical op keeps the original two-child construction. `binary_op`
    is computed once up front (`df_op`) and reused — no behavior change for the
    non-logical path beyond that hoist.
  • sql.rs:45-58 / 63-78 — rewritten from borrow-then-`.clone()` to let-chains
    that MOVE the expr out of the AST (`*query.body`, `select.selection`,
    `select.projection.into_iter().next()`). Removes the deep AST clone the body
    cites as a contributing cause. Behaviorally equivalent on the happy path;
    the new projection path via `into_iter().next()` is strictly safer than the
    old `s.projection[0]` index (no panic on an empty projection).
  • Edition 2024, MSRV 1.91.0 (workspace Cargo.toml @ head) — let-chains
    (stable 1.88) are within MSRV. CI reported green (compiles + tests pass).
  • COVERED: planner.rs:1177 test_parse_deep_logical_filter builds a 1000-wide
    flat AND chain and a 1000-wide flat OR chain, parses + optimizes, asserts
    `lit(true)`. This is exactly the OSS-1232 shape and would overflow on main.
  • NOT COVERED: (a) mixed/alternating operators — `a AND b OR c AND b OR …`;
    (b) deeply parenthesized input — `(((…)))`, which hits SQLExpr::Nested
    (planner.rs:773) and recurses through parse_sql_expr per level. The
    flattening only collapses *same-operator* chains, so these shapes still
    recurse O(depth) and remain vulnerable. I did NOT find a test or a depth
    guard covering them; per the body they are deferred to the follow-up.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: low-med · urgency: low
  Net: a correctness/robustness fix on the SQL planning path, well-scoped, with
  a regression test that reproduces the reported overflow. No format/IO/wire
  surface, no unsafe, no concurrency. The residual recursion on mixed-operator
  and nested-parenthesis inputs is a real but explicitly-deferred limitation,
  not introduced by this PR — it does not gate this focused fix.
  escalation: none (no forced rules)
  could not read: none (0 omitted; both patches full, untruncated)

DRAFT COMMENTS
  praise: flatten_logical_binary_exprs + balanced_binary_expr cleanly remove the
    recursion for the reported case, and the regression test reproduces the
    1000-deep overflow. Moving the expr out of the AST in sql.rs (instead of the
    prior deep clone) is a nice second-order win.

  issue(non-blocking): The fix only protects *same-operator* chains. Alternating
    operators (`a AND b OR c AND d OR …`) and deeply parenthesized filters
    (SQLExpr::Nested, planner.rs:773) still recurse through parse_sql_expr per
    level and can overflow. The PR body already commits to a follow-up depth
    limit — fine to land this as-is; flagging so the residual path is tracked,
    not forgotten. acceptance: author's call (track via the follow-up issue).

  suggestion: Add a test (or a `#[should_panic]`/error-returning case) for a
    deep *alternating-operator* and a deep *nested-parenthesis* filter, so the
    boundary of what this PR does and does not protect is documented in code and
    the eventual depth-limit work has a target. acceptance: author's call.

  question: Is OSS-1232's reproducer a flat same-operator chain (matching
    test_parse_deep_logical_filter), or did it involve nesting/mixed operators?
    If the latter, the fix may not fully close the report. Non-gating — the
    drafted recommendation assumes the flat-chain reproducer the body describes.

RECOMMENDATION: approve (0 blocking conditions)
  confidence: high for the same-operator path (mechanism traced, regression test
    reproduces and passes, CI green). 
  what would make this wrong: if OSS-1232's actual reproducer is mixed-operator
    or deeply nested rather than a flat same-op chain — then this fix is
    necessary but not sufficient. That is the open question above; it lowers
    completeness, not correctness, so it does not convert to a blocking gate.
